Taxonomy_x: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Porifera; Demospongiae; Heteroscleromorpha; Agelasida; Agelasidae; Agelas
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Porifera; Demospongiae; Heteroscleromorpha; Agelasida; Agelasidae; Agelas
Assembly: The assembly odAgeClat2.hap1.1 is based on 101x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Aquatic Symbiosis Genomics Project (https://www.aquaticsymbiosisgenomics.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi and OATK. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Taxonomy_x: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Porifera; Demospongiae; Heteroscleromorpha; Agelasida; Agelasidae; Agelas
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Porifera; Demospongiae; Heteroscleromorpha; Agelasida; Agelasidae; Agelas
Assembly: The assembly odAgeTubu1.1 is based on 22x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Aquatic Symbiosis Genomics Project (https://www.aquaticsymbiosisgenomics.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. A haplotypic inversion was observed in the region on chromosome 3 ( 12.28 - 13.3 Mbp). Chromosome 1 (24.9 Mbp to end) is a potential haplotypic region.

The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.
